verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a single-pass type II TM glycoprotein canonically resident in the trans-Golgi, where its TM domain serves as a Golgi-retention anchor for glycoconjugate biosynthesis. However, UniProt/GeneCards explicitly annotates a 'Cell membrane; Single-pass type II membrane protein' localization, noting the processed form is 'mainly localized at the plasma membrane.' The cell-surface pool — anchored by its own TM domain with the catalytic (luminal/extracellular-facing) domain exposed — mediates cell-cell recognition and sperm-egg interactions. This constitutes a documented dual localization: Golgi-dominant biosynthetic role plus a stable, TM-anchored PM pool accessible from the extracellular face.
